preview_text:
Spain enters as a heavy favourite, with the model assigning a 71% win probability against Uruguay's modest 8%. The gap reflects a substantial quality differential in the statistical forecast. Uruguay's path to victory remains plausible but unlikely from a pure modelling perspective.

The draw carries meaningful weight at 21%, suggesting this fixture will not be decided by a rout in most simulations. Spain's advantage is decisive rather than overwhelming; the underdog retains a combined 29% chance to avoid defeat. The match is expected to be competitive enough that a share of the points ranks as a credible outcome, even if a Spain victory dominates the probability distribution.

The market consensus diverges meaningfully from the model. Sportsbooks and prediction markets both assign Uruguay roughly double the win probability the model does—around 16% versus 8%—while compressing Spain's chances to 61–62%. The draw estimates are tightly aligned across all three sources. The model's heavier confidence in Spain's superiority is the key point of disagreement; the dashboard makes no claim as to which assessment of relative strength is correct.
